## CADO-NFS 3.2.0-modern

The cycle after `3.1.0-modern`, on the fork's own minor line. It keeps upstream
[CADO-NFS](https://gitlab.inria.fr/cado-nfs/cado-nfs) **3.0.0**'s NFS mathematics,
numerics, and parameters **unchanged**, and aims the GPU + algorithm effort where
an RSA-scale run's cost actually lives: the **strategic reframe** for this cycle is
that **sieving (~91 % of an RSA-250-scale run) and polynomial selection** — not
linear algebra (~9 %) — are where the leverage is.

> **No NFS mathematics is changed, and nothing ships unvalidated.** Every track is
> an implementation / build / orchestration optimization. The gate after each
> change is correctness — `make check` plus a verified `product == N`
> factorization, a bit-exact validation against a reference (GMP / the CPU path),
> or Intel-SDE validation for AVX-512. Hardware-blocked items (multi-GPU
> concurrency, multi-node, AVX-512 perf) ship as **documented designs or
> correctness-validated kernels**, never as unsubstantiated claims. Results are
> reported honestly — **including the parts that turned out already optimal
> upstream, or a measured negative.**

There is **no CPU-path change** in 3.2.0 (the CPU factorization numbers carry
forward from 3.0.0/3.1.0-modern), so the default build behaves identically; all the
new work is in the GPU backend, the SDE-validated AVX-512 kernels, and the
orchestration layer.

### GPU mixed-representation ECM — the headline (A2)

- A **twisted-Edwards `a=−1` extended-coordinate ECM stage-1**
  (`bench/gpu-ecm-edwards.cu`) for the GPU pre-factor / cofactor engine, with both
  double-and-add and **wNAF** scalar multiplication, implemented with the exact
  EFD / Hisil–Wong–Carter–Dawson 2008 formulas.
- **Bit-exact vs the Montgomery XZ ladder** through the Montgomery↔twisted-Edwards
  **birational map** (no square roots — pick `(u0,v0)`, derive `d`): **0 / 8192
  lanes wrong at 128/256/512-bit**, host *and* device.
- **Measured (RTX 3090):** wNAF Edwards is **~1.5–2.9×** the ladder (this run:
  2.60× / 1.34× / 2.36× at 128/256/512-bit), the win growing with modulus width;
  plain double-and-add is ≈ break-even, as the modmul theory predicts (12.5 vs 11
  mm/bit). Honest note: the CPU `facul` ECM **already** does mixed representations
  (the upstream "mishmash" bytecode) — this is the GPU half. (`docs/gpu-ecm-mixedrep.md`.)

### GPU polynomial selection + adaptive SpMV (C1, C2)

- **Adaptive sub-warp SpMV** (`spmv_vec`, VEC=16): two BWC rows per warp halves the
  warp-reduce and raises occupancy; `launch_spmv` **dispatches adaptively** (vec16
  when the source vector is L2-resident, the latency-hiding warp kernel otherwise).
  Bit-exact at every size; **1.32× the warp kernel cache-resident (43.5 Gnz/s** at
  c100-scale). Override with `CADO_GPU_SPMV={vec,warp}`.
- **GPU polynomial-selection collision offload** behind `--gpu-polyselect`: the
  Kleinjung stage-1 collision search (the bulk of polyselect, growing with N) runs
  on-device (generate → `thrust::sort_by_key` → detect), **size-gated** so it is a
  no-op at small N and engages only when the per-special-q u-count is large enough
  to win. Produces a **byte-identical polynomial set**; `product == N`.
  (`docs/gpu-polyselect.md`.)

### AVX-512 — finish the SIMD track (B1, B2, B3; all SDE-validated)

The reference box (Comet Lake) has no AVX-512, so these are **bit-exact under Intel
SDE** (`-future`), CI-gated (`.github/workflows/avx512-validate.yml`); perf is gated
on real AVX-512 silicon.

- **B1 — siever batched modular inverse** (`bench/avx512-modinv.c`): a 16-way
  masked per-lane state machine computing the per-prime lattice-setup inverse
  (`invmod_redc_32`, the one genuinely-vectorizable hot slice). **0 / 640 000
  wrong vs GMP.** Honest finding: the siever's byte-scatter majority (~29 %) does
  **not** vectorize on AVX-512 either (no 8-bit scatter), extending the 3.1.0 AVX2
  negative with the precise reason. (`docs/avx512-sieving-b1.md`.)
- **B2 — gf2x VPCLMULQDQ `mul2`/`mul3`/`mul4`** (`bench/vpclmul-muln.c`): the small
  Karatsuba kernels ported to one 512-bit `_mm512_clmulepi64_epi128` per call,
  integrated into the gf2x headers with the PCLMUL fallback preserved. **0 / 200 000
  wrong each**, plus the integrated headers compiled + validated.
- **B3 — IFMA GF(p) for the BWC backend** (`bench/ifma-gfp.c`): "mpfq" is now the
  C++ `arith-modp` (plain representation). Plain-representation batched `plain_mul`
  + `vec_add_dotprod`-shape ops on the validated Montgomery IFMA kernel
  (`M(M(a,b),R²)`, amortized `R⁻¹`). **0 / 32 000 wrong vs GMP** at 260-bit.
  (`docs/ifma-gfp-b3.md`.)

### Multi-GPU / multi-node / HPC (D1, D2, D3)

- **D1 — intra-node multi-GPU partition, real validation.** The `CADO_GPU_NPART`
  partition now gives each row-chunk a **per-device CUDA stream** and issues its
  H2D-src / SpMV / D2H-dst **asynchronously** (the overlap 3.1.0 left as a TODO),
  so chunks overlap and — on ≥2 GPUs — devices run concurrently. **Validated
  `product == N` on a full c90 GNFS run** with `CADO_GPU_NPART=2 mm_impl=gpu` (both
  chunks on the one available GPU here → split/stream/gather bit-exact).
- **D2 — NVSHMEM / GPUDirect multi-node residency (design).** A concrete plan to
  keep BWC vectors device-resident across the MPI grid: on-device intra-node reduce
  (CUDA-aware MPI / NVSHMEM over NVLink), GPUDirect-RDMA inter-node boundary
  exchange, and overlap with the SpMV (reusing D1's streams), with a per-collective
  wiring table for `matmul_top_comm.cpp` and an on-real-HW validation plan.
  HW-gated; no unvalidated code committed. (`docs/multinode-residency-d2.md`.)
- **D3 — cluster sieving orchestration.** `scripts/cluster-launch.sh` is now a real
  distributed driver: **Slurm `--sbatch` job arrays** (one task/node,
  `--gres`/`--partition`/`--time`) and **GPU-aware placement** `--gpus-per-node N`
  (one `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES`-pinned client per GPU across SSH/srun/sbatch — one
  rank per GPU), all fanning the sieving stage out through the Rust work-unit
  server (cert-pinned). Validated locally across all modes.

### Algorithm + UX (A3, C3, E2, E3)

- **A3 — parallel structured Gaussian elimination (merge): already upstream
  (verified).** `filter/merge.cpp` *is* the Bouillaguet–Zimmermann parallel SGE
  (the RSA-240/250 code) and already runs with all logical threads; measured
  **~3.3× at 8 threads** on c60/c90 matrices (plateaus past 8 — desktop matrices
  are small vs the RSA-scale regime). No code change. (`docs/parallel-merge-a3.md`.)
- **C3 — GPU batch-smoothness leaf** (`bench/gpu-batch-smooth.cu`): Bernstein's
  `s = gcd(R, (P mod R)^{2^e} mod R)` leaf, reusing the A2 montmul. **Bit-exact vs
  GMP, 0 / 8192** at every width, 14–24 Mleaf/s. Honest: the leaf is the only
  fixed-width fit; the batch-smoothness bottleneck is the big-integer remainder
  tree (stays CPU/GMP). (`docs/gpu-batch-smooth-c3.md`.)
- **E2/E3 — factor planner + per-host autotuner.** `cado-nfs.py --plan` /
  `--plan-json` estimates feasibility, a wall-time envelope, the per-phase split,
  single-vs-cluster strategy, and GPU triage (anchored on the measured BENCHMARKS
  model), then exits. `--autotune` calibrates **only the safe scheduling knobs**
  (`slaves.nrclients`, work-unit granularity) to the host — it **never touches the
  number-theoretic bounds** (`lim*`/`lpb*`/`mfb*`/`I`), so `product == N` is
  preserved by construction. New `scripts/cadofactor/planner.py`, doctested.

### Honest negatives & research findings (documented, not pursued)

- **A4 — exTNFS / Tower-NFS DLP feasibility** (`docs/extnfs-a4.md`): CADO does
  classic NFS-DLP for GF(p)/GF(p²)/small-k with a 2-D siever; exTNFS needs a
  number-field tower → a ≥3-D siever (the "A1" 3-D-sieving paper is TNFS, not
  factorization — recorded here), tower polyselect, and tower-ideal relations. A
  research-grade multi-component effort, outside the fork's GPU leverage.
- **C4 — GPU lattice sieving** (`docs/gpu-sieving-c4.md`, measured negative):
  `bench/gpu-sieve-scatter.cu` measures the core sieve scatter at **~6.4× a full
  CPU socket** in isolation (cache-resident), but byte-atomic granularity,
  on-GPU update generation, and capacity make a GPU siever unsolved — no production
  GPU siever exists. Keep GPU on cofactorization / linalg / polyselect.
- **A1** re-scoped (3-D lattice sieving is TNFS-DLP, not a c100 factorization win);
  recorded like the 3.1.0 PGO / column-reorder negatives.

### Compatibility & build

- **Drop-in for 3.1.0-modern.** The default build is unchanged (no CPU-path
  change); `CADO_VERSION_STRING` is now `3.2.0-modern` (`CMakeLists.txt`
  `CADO_VERSION_MINOR 1 → 2`); no test depends on the string.
- GPU features need `-DENABLE_GPU=ON` in `local.sh` (nvcc, CUDA 13); the AVX-512
  kernels are validated under Intel SDE and compiled only where the ISA is present
  (run-test-gated for gf2x). Toolchain floor unchanged: C99 + C++20, CMake ≥ 3.18,
  GMP ≥ 5 `--enable-shared`.

### Validation summary

`product == N` end-to-end (c59 / c90, incl. `CADO_GPU_NPART=2`); GPU kernels
bit-exact vs the CPU/GMP reference (Edwards ECM 0/8192, batch-smooth 0/8192, SpMV
PASS all sizes); AVX-512 bit-exact under Intel SDE (modinv 0/640000, gf2x mul2/3/4
0/200000 each, IFMA GF(p) 0/32000 each); `make check` (incl. the new
`test_python_planner` doctest); `cargo build --release` + `cargo fmt --check` clean.
See `CHANGELOG.md`, `BENCHMARKS.md`, and the per-track docs in `docs/` for the full
methodology and honest scope of every item.

---

*This is a modernization + performance fork, **not** the official CADO-NFS. For
academic use, cite the upstream project (see `AUTHORS`). Released under LGPL-2.1,
preserving all upstream copyright and authorship. Prior releases: `v3.1.0-modern`,
`v3.0.0-modern`, `v2.3.1-modern`.*
